name: Scharzhofberg
description: One of Germany's greatest vineyards. Steep slate slopes producing racy, mineral-driven Rieslings with extraordinary aging potential.
historicalNotes: Owned by Egon Müller, von Kesselstatt, and a few others. Egon Müller's wines regularly command highest prices in Germany.

name: Wehlener Sonnenuhr
description: Iconic vineyard with sundial landmark. Produces elegant, racy Rieslings with intense minerality.
historicalNotes: Name means 'sundial of Wehlen.' Joh. Jos. Prüm, Dr. Loosen, Markus Molitor make benchmark wines.

name: Ürziger Würzgarten
description: Unique red volcanic soil produces spicy, exotic Rieslings. 'Würzgarten' means spice garden.
historicalNotes: Distinct terroir creates particularly aromatic wines. Christoffel, Mönchhof, Dr. Loosen excel.

name: Erdener Prälat
description: Tiny, legendary vineyard producing some of Mosel's most powerful and concentrated Rieslings.
historicalNotes: Dr. Loosen and Markus Molitor make exceptional examples. Among the steepest vineyards in Europe.

name: Erdener Treppchen
description: Steep terraced vineyard producing powerful, complex Rieslings. Name means 'little stairway.'
historicalNotes: Carved terraces create dramatic vineyard landscape. Christoffel, Dr. Loosen own key parcels.

name: Brauneberger Juffer Sonnenuhr
description: Historic vineyard producing elegant, mineral Rieslings with great depth.
historicalNotes: Fritz Haag makes iconic examples. One of Mosel's most consistent top sites.

name: Marienburg Fahrlay
description: Single-owner monopole producing powerful, mineral-driven Rieslings with distinctive terroir.
historicalNotes: Monopole of Weingut Clemens Busch. Farmed biodynamically. Among steepest vineyards in Europe.

name: Berg Schlossberg
description: Steepest vineyard in Rheingau. Rocky soil produces powerful, mineral Rieslings.
historicalNotes: Georg Breuer made this site famous. Now part of Theresa Breuer estate.

name: Schloss Johannisberger
description: Historic monopole producing classic, elegant Rieslings. Birthplace of Spätlese.
historicalNotes: Owned by Schloss Johannisberg estate since 1720s. First Riesling planted here in 1720.

name: Steinberg
description: Famous walled vineyard producing structured, age-worthy Rieslings. One of Germany's historic 'first growths.'
historicalNotes: Established by Cistercian monks in 1200s. Owned by Kloster Eberbach state domain.

name: Marcobrunn
description: Legendary small vineyard producing rich, powerful Rieslings. Deep marl soil unique in Rheingau.
historicalNotes: Named after boundary fountain (Markbrunnen). Von Simmern, Knyphausen own key parcels.

name: Nonnenberg
description: High-elevation site producing taut, mineral Rieslings with great aging potential.
historicalNotes: Georg Breuer pioneered dry Riesling from this site. Now farmed by Theresa Breuer.

name: Pechstein
description: Black basalt soil produces exotic, spicy Rieslings. Warmest microclimate in Pfalz.
historicalNotes: Name means 'basalt stone.' Georg Mosbacher, Reichsrat von Buhl excel here.

name: Ungeheuer
description: Largest of Forst's top sites. Name means 'monster.' Produces powerful, complex Rieslings.
historicalNotes: Bassermann-Jordan, von Buhl, Bürklin-Wolf make benchmark wines.

name: Kirchenstück
description: Most prestigious site in Forst. Produces elegant, mineral Rieslings with great finesse.
historicalNotes: Considered one of Germany's finest vineyards. Bürklin-Wolf, von Buhl own key parcels.

name: Freundstück
description: Produces rich, concentrated Rieslings with excellent structure.
historicalNotes: Von Winning, Georg Mosbacher make excellent examples.

name: Gaisböhl
description: Limestone-rich soil produces elegant, mineral Rieslings.
historicalNotes: Von Buhl, Bassermann-Jordan own significant parcels.

name: Kalkofen
description: Name means 'lime kiln.' Calcareous soil produces taut, mineral wines.
historicalNotes: Bassermann-Jordan, von Buhl make structured, age-worthy examples.

name: Hohenmorgen
description: High-elevation site producing elegant, complex Rieslings.
historicalNotes: Bürklin-Wolf, von Buhl, Bassermann-Jordan all own parcels.

name: Rüdesheimer Berg Roseneck
description: Steep, rocky slopes producing powerful, mineral Rieslings.
historicalNotes: Georg Breuer, Leitz make outstanding examples from this challenging site.
